name: Raisin Soda Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Grease a 9-inch cast-iron skillet or cake pan and dust with flour.
3. In a large bowl, mix 4 cups of flour with baking powder, baking soda, salt, sugar, and caraway seeds. Add raisins and mix until evenly distributed.
4. Add buttermilk to the bowl and mix with a fork until a dough forms.
5. Transfer dough to a floured surface or cutting board. Knead for about 5 minutes until smooth and round, approximately 8 inches in diameter. If needed, add more flour to prevent sticking.
6. Press dough into prepared skillet or pan. Cut a cross 1/2 inch deep across the top.
7. Bake for 1 hour and 15 minutes, or until hollow when tapped on bottom. Remove from oven and let cool on wire rack.
8. Rub cooled bread with softened butter and sprinkle with sugar. Slice before serving.
This Irish bread tastes even better the second day and is particularly good for breakfast.
